title: Tgs Hates Women

jokes.text: Wonderful news, non-famouses.
jokes.punchline: Condescending term 'non-famouses' reveals character's narcissism
jokes.explanation: Perfect character work - only Jenna would address people this way. Economical and establishes her worldview instantly.

jokes.text: My publicist just called from rehab.
jokes.punchline: Casual mention that publicist is in rehab
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing the dysfunction in Jenna's world delivered casually.

jokes.text: It's this really cool feminist web site where women talk about how far we've come and which celebrities have the worst beach bodies.
jokes.punchline: Contradictory purposes - empowerment vs body shaming
jokes.explanation: Sharp observation about feminist hypocrisy in media. Well-structured contradiction.

jokes.text: Ruth Bader Ginsburg!
jokes.setup: Previous discussion about celebrity beach bodies
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate celebrity crush/reference to Supreme Court justice
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly unexpected and economical. Perfect non-sequitur that somehow makes sense for Jenna.

jokes.text: Take that, Courtney Thorne-Smith.
jokes.punchline: Random rivalry with irrelevant celebrity from decades ago
jokes.explanation: Perfect character beat - Jenna's weird competitive nature with random celebrities.

jokes.text: Computer, 'Jenna'!
jokes.punchline: Talking to computer like voice assistant, searching for herself
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Jenna's narcissism and tech illiteracy.

jokes.text: This is Amelia Earhart. I'm almost across the Pacific... Oh, no. My period!
jokes.punchline: Reducing aviation pioneer to menstrual emergency
jokes.explanation: Absurd reduction of historical figure, though borders on cheap shock humor.

jokes.text: I'll now take questions. Oh, no. My period!
jokes.punchline: Same period joke repeated
jokes.explanation: Repetitive beat that doesn't add much beyond establishing the pattern.

jokes.text: Let's nuke England!
jokes.punchline: Completely unrelated violent non-sequitur
jokes.explanation: Random escalation that's absurd but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: But that is an ironic reappropriation.
jokes.punchline: Using academic buzzwords to justify bad comedy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz character moment - overthinking and intellectualizing to avoid admitting failure.

jokes.text: Absolutely. But it's not your fault. You are genetically predisposed to compete against other women for the attention of strong, powerful men like myself
jokes.setup: Liz asking if Jack thinks she hates women
jokes.punchline: Jack's immediate 'yes' followed by pseudo-scientific mansplaining
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Jack moment - confident wrongness delivered with authority. Perfect character integration.

jokes.text: For example, Hercules, the highlander, or, uh...God.
jokes.setup: Jack listing examples of powerful men
jokes.punchline: Mix of mythical, fictional, and divine beings as if equivalent
jokes.explanation: Great escalation and category error that perfectly captures Jack's grandiosity.

jokes.text: I want to roll my eyes right now, but the doctor said if I keep doing it my ocular muscles might spasm and eject my eyeballs.
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous medical condition preventing eye rolling
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist solution to show character's frustration while being visually funny.

jokes.text: If you try to breed it out of them, you end up with a lesbian with hip dysplasia.
jokes.setup: Jack talking about female jealousy as evolutionary
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous pseudo-scientific outcome
jokes.explanation: Absurd Jack logic that's character-appropriate but borders on offensive stereotyping.

jokes.text: I'm like a human bra,
jokes.punchline: Awkward metaphor comparing herself to undergarment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz moment - earnest but awkward metaphor that accidentally sounds sexual.

jokes.text: Please, Lemon. It's got nothing to do with her hot mouth.
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicts himself with sexual comment
jokes.explanation: Good character contradiction - Jack's inability to not objectify women even when trying.

jokes.text: Like Hank Hooper says in his book, 'New blood is the lifeblood of every company's blood.'
jokes.punchline: Redundant, nonsensical use of 'blood' three times
jokes.explanation: Solid wordplay joke about corporate speak being meaningless.

jokes.text: He's not a strong writer.
jokes.punchline: Massive understatement about obvious poor writing
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery and massive understatement creates good laugh.

jokes.text: Isn't that the guy who outbid no one for NBC?
jokes.punchline: Implying NBC was so worthless no one else wanted it
jokes.explanation: Excellent self-deprecating joke about the network. Sharp and economical.

jokes.text: I read that on a bottle of women's exercise water.
jokes.punchline: Jack reveals source as absurdly specific product
jokes.explanation: Great specificity in the absurd product name. Sets up callback later.

jokes.text: Hi, I'm Kaylie Hooper. I'm 14 years old and I'm waiting in line to meet Justin Bieber 'cause he's...
jokes.punchline: Reveal that she's a teenage Bieber fan
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the character dynamic - corporate threat reduced to teen girl.

jokes.text: Your new rival is a ninth grade girl?
jokes.punchline: Liz's disbelief at the absurd situation
jokes.explanation: Good straight-man reaction to highlight the absurdity.

jokes.text: Her parents' generation was lost to 'trust fund kids' disease.
jokes.punchline: Made-up disease for rich kid dysfunction
jokes.explanation: Clever creation of fake medical condition that rings true for wealthy families.

jokes.text: The father is trying to sail an inflatable castle across the Atlantic.
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous maritime adventure with wrong equipment
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly specific absurd image that perfectly captures rich person delusion.

jokes.text: To become a doctor's nurse or a lawyer's mistress or even the president of the United States Shopping Association.
jokes.setup: Jack listing career options for Kaylie
jokes.punchline: Fake-empowering titles that are actually diminishing
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation that reveals Jack's retrograde views while sounding progressive.

jokes.text: Because potential is the difference between what you can't do and what can't you do.
jokes.punchline: Grammatically nonsensical motivational phrase
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz moment - trying too hard to be profound and failing grammatically.

jokes.text: No, I sent it in, but Aquafem did not choose it.
jokes.setup: Jack asking if she got phrase from water bottle
jokes.punchline: Liz admits submitting bad motivational quotes to water company
jokes.explanation: Great callback that adds depth to Liz's character - she actually does this stuff.

jokes.text: Girl coming!
jokes.punchline: Treating woman's arrival like emergency alert
jokes.explanation: Good setup for writer room dynamics and their awkwardness with women.

jokes.text: I mean, light travels at 186,000 miles per second, so...
jokes.setup: Question about how long it takes to see something
jokes.punchline: Literal scientific answer to rhetorical question
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Toofer's overthinking nature.

jokes.text: 'Cause chicks dig British guys. Sir Ian McKellen? That dude must be knee-deep in boob.
jokes.setup: Frank explaining appeal of British accent
jokes.punchline: Horrible example using elderly gay actor
jokes.explanation: Perfectly wrong example that shows Frank's cluelessness about everything.

jokes.text: Star jump! I found the chrysalis!
jokes.setup: Threat of Lutz with Xbox Kinect shirtless
jokes.punchline: Lutz playing motion game with nature documentary enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag that captures Lutz's weird enthusiasm and physical comedy.

jokes.text: But what most people don't know is that NBC is still a network.
jokes.punchline: Self-deprecating joke about network's irrelevance
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke about the show's network. Perfect economy and self-awareness.

jokes.text: What you talkin' 'bout, Kaylie? School's cool. Just like Justin 'Bee-eye-bear.'
jokes.setup: Jack trying to relate to teenager
jokes.punchline: Awkward slang attempts and mispronouncing 'Bieber'
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe moment showing Jack's inability to connect with youth culture.

jokes.text: So our new mascot is a slut.
jokes.punchline: Unexpected reveal after discussing fish
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection and economy. Unexpected dark turn from innocent setup.

jokes.text: He discovered the Titanic, the Lusitania, and according to his website, a guilt-free cheesecake recipe.
jokes.punchline: Mundane recipe discovery among historical shipwrecks
jokes.explanation: Good escalation joke mixing historical significance with domestic triviality.

jokes.text: Okay, now, please follow me to Brian Williams' bathroom, which is also J. Fred Muggs' skull.
jokes.punchline: NBC bathroom doubles as chimp skull memorial
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd callback that escalates the NBC weirdness to perfect extreme.

jokes.text: West ham drew nil-nil at wolves?
jokes.punchline: Random soccer score as attempt at British conversation
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Lutz's shallow understanding of British culture.

jokes.text: Don't you know I'm talkin' 'bout a femolution?
jokes.punchline: Made-up word combining 'female' and 'revolution'
jokes.explanation: Decent portmanteau that captures Liz's awkward attempts at feminism.

jokes.text: Look at all these dudes! I knew I smelled sausage.
jokes.punchline: Sexual innuendo about male-dominated workspace
jokes.explanation: Establishes Abby's hypersexualized character but relies on obvious innuendo.

jokes.text: Our nips just touched. Mine are so hard. Mine are different sizes.
jokes.punchline: Immediate oversharing about nipples
jokes.explanation: Character establishment through shock value but feels somewhat cheap.

jokes.text: Oh, where's little Abby gonna sit? Guess I'll have to sit on somebody's lap.
jokes.punchline: Abby's baby talk and sexual suggestion
jokes.explanation: Establishes character but relies on predictable sexy baby trope.

jokes.text: Give me strength, oh, Oprah.
jokes.punchline: Praying to Oprah instead of God
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz moment - treating Oprah as deity fits character's liberal feminism.

jokes.text: When I first started working here, an eight-year-old Shirley Temple taught me how to roll a cigarette.
jokes.punchline: Child star teaching adult executive to smoke
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd NBC history that's both nostalgic and inappropriate.

jokes.text: This guy.
jokes.punchline: Pointing to himself with complete confidence
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack confidence and self-promotion. Economical and in-character.

jokes.text: Practice frottage on a poster of Linda Ronstadt and meet your idol.
jokes.setup: Jack explaining what teenage science geeks want
jokes.punchline: Sexual activity with dated celebrity poster
jokes.explanation: Inappropriately specific and dated reference that's funny but uncomfortable.

jokes.text: Oh, I did forget. It was so long ago.
jokes.setup: Jack saying he'll never forget meeting Cousteau
jokes.punchline: Immediate admission he forgot
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic timing and contradiction. Good structure and character beat.

jokes.text: Word choice, Jack.
jokes.punchline: Liz calling out sexual innuendo
jokes.explanation: Good meta-moment where character acknowledges inappropriate language.

jokes.text: Is it cold in here or is it just that I'm not wearing any underwear?
jokes.punchline: Unexpected personal revelation instead of 'nipples hard'
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection on familiar phrase but still relies on sexual shock value.

jokes.text: New York gives us a tax break for employing sex offenders. It's a terrible program.
jokes.setup: Warning about crew around new woman
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of horrible employment program
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark joke about bureaucratic dysfunction. Perfect deadpan delivery.

jokes.text: Summer of '98, I took it to a whole new level.
jokes.setup: Jenna claiming to have invented sexy baby act
jokes.punchline: Specific date and pride in perfecting it
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna moment - claiming ownership of questionable behavior with specific details.

jokes.text: There can't be two of us, Liz. She must be destroyed.
jokes.punchline: Dramatic declaration of war over persona
jokes.explanation: Good character escalation showing Jenna's competitive narcissism.

jokes.text: I'll start by spreading a destructive rumor about her like the famous one I made up about Jamie Lee Curtis. That she has two butts.
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous celebrity rumor about extra anatomy
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd and specific rumor that's perfectly ridiculous yet believable for Jenna.

jokes.text: You're not. 'Bob' is short for 'Bobert.'
jokes.setup: Kaylie excited to meet Dr. Robert Ballard
jokes.punchline: Made-up longer name for 'Bob'
jokes.explanation: Absurd name logic that's unexpectedly funny through commitment to nonsense.

jokes.text: You know that's a female. The male is much smaller and is basically a parasite that lives on her. Until mating, of course. Then the male dies.
jokes.punchline: Grim biological reality of fish mating
jokes.explanation: Good setup for marriage joke while establishing Kaylie's knowledge.

jokes.text: Yes, the scientific term for that is 'marriage.'
jokes.setup: Description of male anglerfish dying after mating
jokes.punchline: Comparison to human marriage
jokes.explanation: Classic marriage joke structure but feels predictable and cliché.

jokes.text: I'm not gonna live forever, no matter how much gold I give Poseidon.
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of bribing sea god
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd detail that suggests ocean explorer has gone slightly mad.

jokes.text: What if you gave me a sticker as a joke?
jokes.setup: Jack asking about age cutoff for student program
jokes.punchline: Adult man worried about being pranked with children's reward
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Jack's hidden insecurity about his childhood dreams.

jokes.text: There she is, men. The elusive northern right whale.
jokes.setup: Jack caught up in ocean fantasy
jokes.punchline: Jack addressing imaginary crew while looking at whale
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag showing Jack's complete immersion in explorer fantasy.

jokes.text: ♪ Oh blow the wind westerly ♪ ♪ let the wind blow ♪ ♪ oh derry hi derry ♪ ♪ hey derry ho ♪
jokes.punchline: Singing sea shanty at natural history museum
jokes.explanation: Good character moment but the singing goes on a bit long.

jokes.text: Yes, sailor? What is it?
jokes.setup: Liz trying to get Jack's attention
jokes.punchline: Jack fully committed to ship captain role
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Jack's fantasy but somewhat predictable.

jokes.text: Does this look like the makeup room of a clown academy?
jokes.setup: Abby asking if this is where Liz lost virginity
jokes.punchline: Liz's sarcastic response about Eleanor Roosevelt statue
jokes.explanation: Good sarcastic comeback with specific absurd imagery.

jokes.text: My uncle says they're sexy.
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate family member commentary
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable joke that relies on incest implication for shock value.

jokes.text: This is my real voice. And the whole sexy baby thing isn't an act. I'm a very sexy baby.
jokes.setup: Liz asking Abby to drop the act
jokes.punchline: Insistence that disturbing persona is authentic
jokes.explanation: Good character revelation but the concept is inherently uncomfortable.

jokes.text: It's not! It's for her!
jokes.punchline: Liz's defensive reaction about who he's looking at
jokes.explanation: Decent character beat showing Liz's insecurity but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: I placed out of freshman German.
jokes.punchline: Modest academic achievement as proof of intelligence
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz moment - defensive overstatement of minor achievement.

jokes.text: I mean, I was there when he belvedered.
jokes.punchline: Using 'Belvedere' (brand name) as sexual verb
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay creating sexual verb from fancy brand name. Very economical.

jokes.text: I hope my legacy is a Sesame Street type TV show that promotes illiteracy in girls.
jokes.punchline: Educational show with opposite purpose
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark Kenneth joke - sounds innocent but is deeply wrong. Great character integration.

jokes.text: And we walruses owe him everything for giving us the gift of language.
jokes.punchline: Fantasy about teaching walruses to speak
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd fantasy that captures Jack's grandiosity perfectly.

jokes.text: Well, that's understandable. I mean, 'Grossman' is a little bagel-y.
jokes.setup: Learning Abby changed her name from Grossman
jokes.punchline: Anti-Semitic stereotype delivered casually
jokes.explanation: Character-appropriate but relies on ethnic stereotype. Shows Jenna's casual prejudice.

jokes.text: Oh. Children's book idea: Baby hooker. Don't tell Liz.
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate children's book concept
jokes.explanation: Dark Jenna moment that's absurd but shows her twisted creative mind.

jokes.text: This is Charles. I'm on for Douglas. No, Charles. I'm on, but I just dialed in.
jokes.punchline: Confusing overlap of people with same name
jokes.explanation: Good observational comedy about corporate call confusion.

jokes.text: This picture exists in only two places. The negative is in my personal safe along with my will and some beanie babies that I thought would be worth more.
jokes.setup: Jack discovering Kaylie has his photo
jokes.punchline: Revealing worthless collectibles in important safe
jokes.explanation: Excellent character detail showing Jack's investment mistakes alongside important documents.

jokes.text: You're just like Vanessa from the 'Vampire Detective Mysteries.'
jokes.setup: Kaylie calling Jack a super sleuth
jokes.punchline: Reference to made-up teenage book series
jokes.explanation: Good character beat with believable teen book reference.

jokes.text: I breathe it.
jokes.setup: Kaylie explaining her business world advantage
jokes.punchline: Dramatic overstatement of corporate immersion
jokes.explanation: Good dramatic teenage overstatement but not particularly original.

jokes.text: 'Cause I can always tell Pop-Pop you gave me alcohol. And I can always seduce one of your teachers and get her to fail you.
jokes.setup: Kaylie and Jack threatening each other
jokes.punchline: Escalating from teenage tattling to inappropriate adult behavior
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing both characters' willingness to fight dirty.

jokes.text: I'd be into that. Me too.
jokes.setup: Jack threatening to seduce Kaylie's teacher
jokes.punchline: Both realizing they'd enjoy the scenario
jokes.explanation: Good reaction beat but edges into uncomfortable territory.

jokes.text: You know what sucked about my last lesbian orgy? Right in the middle of it, one of us had to get up to go use the bathroom and then we all had to go!
jokes.punchline: Relatable bathroom phenomenon in inappropriate context
jokes.explanation: Good observational comedy about group psychology in shocking context.

jokes.text: Hey, first of all, Steve Carell owns 'that's what she said.' He owns it.
jokes.punchline: Liz defending intellectual property of catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Great meta-joke about comedy ownership and NBC's relationship with The Office.

jokes.text: I was cut out of that.
jokes.punchline: Jenna making it about herself
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna moment - making someone else's trauma about her career.

jokes.text: I thought this box would be the perfect size for your head. P.S. I was electrocuted again while watching Seven.
jokes.punchline: Another movie electrocution incident
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and callback showing the pattern of movie-related electrocutions.

jokes.text: Where Wonder Woman... Gets her period.
jokes.punchline: Return to same terrible period joke premise
jokes.explanation: Callback showing nothing has changed but the joke is tired by this point.

jokes.text: No, the only thing that will cure ice cream headaches is having sex on a motorcycle.
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous alternative medical advice
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy absurdist logic replacing scientific fact with ridiculous solution.

jokes.text: When a dolphin expresses excitement it sounds like this.
jokes.punchline: Implication he makes dolphin sounds
jokes.explanation: Good character moment but the visual gag is implied rather than shown.

tmdb_overview: Liz hires a new female writer after a blog declares "TGS" misogynistic. Meanwhile, Jack plots his takeover of Kabletown by zeroing in on his main competition--the CEO's granddaughter.
insight: TGS writers weaponize sexism jokes—83 total gags attack the show-within-show's misogyny.
